attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches final acceleration in last 200-500m. Does not initiate early; relies on timing and raw power once positioned on rival wheels.
team_tactics: Team provides lead-outs (Matthews 2022) but Groves often abandons organized train to freelance onto faster rival wheels, showing independent tactical thinking over team structure reliance.
terrain_preference: Flat, pure sprint finishes where raw speed advantage is decisive. Crosswind chaos does not affect him negatively (Catalunya 2022). Exposed by technical finishes requiring precise positioning.
vulnerability: Positional errors in final 500m-2.3km consistently negate his superior raw power. Lead-out coordination issues appear chronic across multiple seasons (2023 Vuelta references). Critical corners in sprint finales.
key_patterns: Highest raw speed documented but repeatedly loses due to poor positioning (2023 Vuelta, recurring theme)
Abandons team lead-out to freelance on faster rival wheels when tactically advantageous (Catalunya 2022)
Positional errors at technical points 500m-2.3km from finish cost results multiple times
Demonstrates tactical intelligence reading rival moves but execution consistency lacking in finale chaos
form_trajectory: neutral: Raw power remains elite (highest speed 2023) but positional/lead-out weaknesses persist unresolved from 2022-2023, suggesting stagnation rather than development
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 19
